enonce.texte: Match the e-commerce terms on the left with their correct definitions on the right to review key vocabulary related to online shopping.

questions.texte: Match the following terms with their definitions.

sousQuestions.reponse: d. A website where goods or services are sold over the internet.
a. A virtual basket where customers collect items before purchasing.
b. The process of paying for items in an online store.
c. A collection of web pages with information or services.
e. A plastic card used to make payments online.

enonce.texte:
Complete the sentences below with the correct words from the box to demonstrate your understanding of online shopping vocabulary.

Words: cart, checkout, website, credit card, transfer, mobile money, online shop

questions.texte: Fill in the blanks with the most appropriate word.
questions.reponse: 1. online shop, 2. cart, 3. checkout, 4. credit card, 5. transfer, 6. mobile money

sousQuestions.texte: I found a great deal on an ______ that sells electronics.
She added the dress to her ______ and proceeded to pay.
The ______ process is very secure on this platform.
Many people use their ______ to shop online.
A bank ______ is a common method of payment in Cameroon.
______ is widely used in Africa for online transactions.

enonce.texte:
Read the text about online shopping in Cameroon and answer the questions that follow.

"Online shopping is becoming increasingly popular in Cameroon, especially in urban areas like Douala and Yaoundé. Many consumers prefer using mobile money services such as Orange Money and MTN Mobile Money for transactions because they are fast and secure. E-commerce platforms like Jumia and Afrimalin offer a variety of products, from electronics to clothing. However, some people still hesitate to shop online due to concerns about delivery delays and product quality. To address these issues, companies are improving their customer service and delivery systems. Experts believe that with better internet connectivity and payment security, online shopping will continue to grow in Cameroon."

questions.texte: What are the main reasons for the growing popularity of online shopping in Cameroon?
questions.reponse: The convenience of mobile money services and the availability of various products on e-commerce platforms.

questions.texte: List two payment methods mentioned in the text.
questions.reponse: Mobile money (Orange Money, MTN Mobile Money) and credit cards.

questions.texte: What are some concerns that prevent people from shopping online?
questions.reponse: Delivery delays and concerns about product quality.

questions.texte: What are companies doing to solve these problems?
questions.reponse: They are improving customer service and delivery systems.

questions.texte: According to the text, what factors will contribute to the future growth of online shopping in Cameroon?
questions.reponse: Better internet connectivity and improved payment security.

enonce.texte:
You recently bought a smartphone from an online store. The product arrived late and does not work properly. Write a formal email to the company's customer service department to complain.

Your email should include:

  • A subject line
  • A polite greeting
  • A clear description of the problem (delivery and product quality)
  • A request for a solution (refund or replacement)
  • A polite closing

questions.texte: Write a formal complaint email using appropriate vocabulary and tone.
questions.reponse: Students should produce a well-structured email with proper salutation, clear explanation of the issue, and polite request for action. They should use e-commerce vocabulary such as order, delivery, refund, replacement, customer service, etc.
